MAJOR overhaul of all macro driven systems have led to a decrease in drawing lag of approximately 350ms per click for drawing rooms and placing windows/doors/cabinets are as follows:

Window/Door/Cabinet label systems have all been turned into global methods and have been re-written to call other global methods. This reduces the time it takes for each window/door/cabinet to pass through their logical arguments.
Global helper macros were created to remove bottlenecks.
The roof and foundation NFA and the Area Analysis systems were all combined into single macros to reduce redundant calls.
Anno tool has been completely re-written for more easily customizable annotations and now automatically reports floor surface when not connected but inside of a room.
Most macro systems are now global lambdas with a reporting output so you can see the output but the call is done through a global which allows objects to retrieve values concurrently.
Removed lesser used or obscure macros for easier management in the TMM console.
Changed order of calls and dependencies to avoid macro checks that were unnecessary.

Rewrote the room and ceiling height macros again and now they automatically format themselves based on the room sizes. The truncate tool now will revert that formatting in the case that you want a full size label to place outside of a small room.
All objects now work by looking at a global label system to start parsing their individual values instead of one-by-one iterating through a macro in the TMM thus waiting its turn for the next.
Overhauled the Detail Designations Page. Added new categories for deck structural elements and others.
Removed the cadlabel fill-based designation system, rewrote details to remove this system.
Built a library of annotations for integrating into the system.
Redesigned the detail designations page to simplify it. The detail preview has now been removed.
New Construction dimension defaults have now been set to centerline of door openings and single side of main wall layer.
Windows now have callouts in elevation and kitchen SPVs instead of labels. Labels exist in every other view type.
New callout tool drives details from being connected to a wall.

Overhaul of the roof settings for a new modern look and feel. Replaced the roof texture with a tintable comp shingle roof, Improved the bump map. Added new fried boards and gable end shadow board moldings. Removed z bar from gable end and changed the overhang. Changed default materials. Added a gutter guard.
Added new casings to windows and doors and a new custom sill profile for a clean look.
Added a new exterior siding texture that looks more realistic.
Added a new concrete texture with ao maps for pronounced shadows.
Aggregated plan materials and reduced some file sizes.
Added new framing back clip for easier cross sections.
Second overhaul of all layer based fill styles and layer sets including overhaul of all built in wall types.
Replaced png files with webp files in the library for better file sizes.
Updated helper macros for schedules that use alphanumeric labels.

Library: The best part of this update is SPEED! I found significant bottlenecks in the macro driven systems. Many macros referenced your user_options through a macro call. What does that mean. It means your cabinets window, doors, and rooms were all standing in line for their turn to check your options, and worse, they forced these options to rebuild every time. I switched to a global macro system that requires no line whatsoever. Your plan file should be a few milliseconds faster per object placed.
There is a tradeoff. You will need to open a cabinet or window before printing if no cabinet or window has been open during your entire drafting session.
Requires that you delete the existing Kitchen Tools Library and empty your trash library and then install the new kitchen tools library.
Rebuilt the existing deck railing detail.
New deck ledger detail with DTT2z lateral load ties.

Can now use globals to call out current code cycle references. More info on the macro designations CAD detail. This means if for instance you are using CBC spec and you put %$building_code% in a detail it will print “2022 CBC”.

Re-built the remap_small_font macro so it can be applied in regular text, schedules, and more. Check the Project preferences for more info. This means rich text like looking outputs in schedules and anywhere where we can’t or don’t want to use rich text.

Window/Door and Cabinet Schedules have been completely rebuilt with the font remapping and look much improved. Enjoy the only Rich-text like schedules on the market. Their columns to include have been renamed for clarity on input location.Their is a custom macro that generates automatic spacing in both the cabinet and window/door labels so that all rows remain spaced equal and keep alignment on schedule wrapping. This makes for very clean schedules that look organized and better than ever.
Added a new square footing stackable detail.
Added a new girder specification rule and quick switch in the macro designations CAD detail.

Library: The Area Map has now been contained within a Pro Plan CAD detail for easier updating of all Layouts.

Kitchen Tools Macro Conflict Mitigation: Major efforts undertaken to resolve macro conflicts—particularly in the kitchen tools package, covering symbols and style palette tools. While some macro conflicts remain in other packages, kitchen tools were the focus of this update.

Annotation Tool Default Values: Introduced new default values for the Annot Tool field in most kitchen tools. For example, the base double bass drawer cabinet’s annotation now automatically reads “pots and pans” for new plan and “demo” for as-built plans when linked to the Annot Tool or default note tool. This logic extends to numerous kitchen style palette tools, streamlining kitchen design annotation and reducing redundant tasks for designers.

Note Tool Enhancement: The note tool was rewritten to recognize annotation types within the Annot Tool field (custom fields in the Object Information panel). Proposed and as-built annotations are now separated by a semicolon (;). For example: pots and pans; demo will show “pots and pans” in a proposed plan and “demo” in an as-built plan.
Base Cabinet Label Improvements: The base cabinet label now intelligently detects cook tops—if a cabinet has an appliance inserted in its top, it is no longer automatically labeled “sink base.”.
Kitchen Tool Enhancements: Various kitchen tools received additional functions and bug fixes, increasing their reliability and feature set.
Fixed Climate Zone macro to report immediately from the CSV file.
Fixed misaligned detail page on Arrow Layout.
The wall cabinet and full height cabinet labels got destroyed by an AI edit and I just now caught it. Returned function for recognizing wall cabinets properly now and added some function as well.
Plan Template: Updated.
CSV system supports proper subolders such as Project name John, Subfolder Kitchen. See screenshot below.
Pro Plan has been reorganized for faster visual recognition of folder structure.
CSV system now has a switch to turn it off and revert to Designer/Client Information based input. You can reformat any field by using a colon to create a name value pair such as.
CSV system now supports periods INSTEAD of “ – “ if the period is present.
Here are examples:
Project Name: ARS – Alex.Project Folders.Test will look in /ARS – Alex/Project Folders/Test.
Project Name: ARS-345 – Test will look in /ARS/Test.
Project Name: Alex – Client-24.Test will look in /Alex – Client/Test.

So if a period is present, logic for space minus space is circumvented. If no period is present, space minus space creates a subfolder. In both cases a minus will omit anything after the minus up until another subfolder rule.
Keynotes have all new logic. For example, the Floor Plan Keynotes will auto change their name to Demo Keynotes if in the AB file. You can change the name in columns to include of the schedule.

Annotation Tool Default Values: Introduced new default values for the Annot Tool field in most kitchen tools. For example, the base double bass drawer cabinet’s annotation now automatically reads “pots and pans” for new plan and “demo” for as-built plans when linked to the Annot Tool or default note tool. This logic extends to numerous kitchen style palette tools, streamlining kitchen design annotation and reducing redundant tasks for designers.

Project CSV File System Update: The project CSV file system now accepts periods (.) in addition to “space-dash-space” for distinguishing subfolders. It also accepts partial matches if it finds no matches as well as a better implementation of searching folder structures. This has not been tested on Mac but should work far better than previous versions.

This change supports broader folder naming conventions, including the use of “space-dash-space” in custom folder names outside Chief Architect. Fuzzy matching was improved to better handle extra spaces in project file names.
Such as John.ADA as a project name will look in the /John/ADA/ folder.
Fixed header label for No Work condition to report N/A to schedule.

The Keynote Schedules now have a new trick implemented. They are using a conditional title for the keynote that changes wether the plan is an existing plan or new plan. For example, the floor plan keynotes will display demo keynotes on your -AB plan.
Layout Files: Guide points have been added to logo replacement and an engineer stamp input has been added to the logo field. Please delete the stamp if you do not use the stamp, this is the easiest way to remove it from all layouts.
The Area Map has now been contained within a Pro Plan CAD detail for easier updating of all Layouts.

The CSV system ignores any file name after the “Layout” folder it exists in, but if your project name is ARS – Alex. And you make a subfolder of your project such as “Kitchen” then the CSV will look in /ARS – Alex/Kitchen/.

Keynotes have all new logic. For example, the Floor Plan Keynotes will auto change their name to Demo Keynotes if in the AB file. You can change the name in columns to include of the schedule.

Kitchen Tools Macro Conflict Mitigation: Major efforts undertaken to resolve macro conflicts—particularly in the kitchen tools package, covering symbols and style palette tools. While some macro conflicts remain in other packages, kitchen tools were the focus of this update.

Annotation Tool Default Values: Introduced new default values for the Annot Tool field in most kitchen tools. For example, the base double bass drawer cabinet’s annotation now automatically reads “pots and pans” for new plan and “demo” for as-built plans when linked to the Annot Tool or default note tool. This logic extends to numerous kitchen style palette tools, streamlining kitchen design annotation and reducing redundant tasks for designers.

Note Tool Enhancement: The note tool was rewritten to recognize annotation types within the Annot Tool field (custom fields in the Object Information panel). Proposed and as-built annotations are now separated by a semicolon (;). For example: pots and pans; demo will show “pots and pans” in a proposed plan and “demo” in an as-built plan.
Kitchen Tool Enhancements: Various kitchen tools received additional functions and bug fixes, increasing their reliability and feature set.

Project CSV File System Update: The project CSV file system now accepts periods (.) in addition to “space-dash-space” for distinguishing subfolders. It also accepts partial matches if it finds no matches as well as a better implementation of searching folder structures. This has not been tested on Mac but should work far better than previous versions.

This change supports broader folder naming conventions, including the use of “space-dash-space” in custom folder names outside Chief Architect. Fuzzy matching was improved to better handle extra spaces in project file names.
Such as John.ADA as a project name will look in the /John/ADA/ folder.
The wall cabinet and full height cabinet labels got destroyed by an AI edit and I just now caught it. Returned function for recognizing wall cabinets properly now and added some function as well.

The Keynote Schedules now have a new trick implemented. They are using a conditional title for the keynote that changes wether the plan is an existing plan or new plan. For example, the floor plan keynotes will display demo keynotes on your -AB plan.

Removed or Replaced: Guide points have been added to logo replacement and an engineer stamp input has been added to the logo field. Please delete the stamp if you do not use the stamp, this is the easiest way to remove it from all layouts.
Base Cabinet Label Improvements: The base cabinet label now intelligently detects cook tops—if a cabinet has an appliance inserted in its top, it is no longer automatically labeled “sink base.”.
